steps.title: Identify the list structure
steps.content: The sentence lists three deserts: the Syrian Desert (ranked ninth), the Kyzylkum Desert (ranked sixteenth), and the Chalbi Desert (ranked twenty-ninth). Each item contains an internal comma between the desert name and its ranking. When list items contain internal commas, semicolons must separate the items.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong punctuation patterns
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'ninth in size, the Kyzylkum Desert; ranked sixteenth,': The semicolon falls between "Desert" and "ranked," splitting the Kyzylkum entry from its own ranking instead of separating it from the Syrian Desert entry. ✗
  • Choice B — 'ninth in size; the Kyzylkum Desert, ranked sixteenth;': Semicolons divide the three desert entries from each other; the comma inside "Kyzylkum Desert, ranked sixteenth" correctly marks the internal pause within that item. ✓
  • Choice C — 'ninth in size the Kyzylkum Desert, ranked sixteenth': No semicolon separates the Syrian Desert entry from the Kyzylkum entry, creating a run-on list. ✗
  • Choice D — 'ninth in size; the Kyzylkum Desert; ranked sixteenth;': The second semicolon splits "the Kyzylkum Desert" from its own ranking description, breaking the item apart. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
